{{short description|User script for adding book and journal links to citations}}

'''BooksUp''' is a user script that finds, and helps you add, links to freely
readable scanned copies of the books and journals cited in an article. Proposed
changes are loaded into the normal edit window so you can review (and modify)
them before saving — nothing is ever saved automatically.

It is the on-wiki front end for the [https://bup.toolforge.org bup tool], which
pre-computes the links; the script reads them through bup's read-only API.

[[File:BooksUp gadget.jpg|thumb|right|320px|The BooksUp panel: suggestions for the
current article, with the proposed link shown in blue.]]

== Installation ==

{{Iusc|User:GreenC/BooksUp.js}}

If you have the [[User:Enterprisey/script-installer|script-installer]] gadget,
click the '''install''' button above. Otherwise, add the line to your
[[Special:MyPage/common.js|common.js]] and save, then bypass your browser cache
({{Key press|Ctrl|Shift|R}}).

== Usage ==

Open any article; a '''BooksUp''' link appears in the ''Tools'' menu (it is also
available while editing the wikitext). Click it to open the BooksUp panel.

=== This article ===

Any suggestions for the current article are listed, each showing the '''current'''
citation and the '''proposed''' one. The added link is shown in blue — click it to
open the scanned copy in a new tab and confirm it is the right source and page.

* Use the '''Add''' / '''Skip''' buttons under each suggestion to choose which to
  apply.
* Click '''Open in editor''' to load the accepted changes into the edit window
  (or '''Apply to editor''' if you are already editing). Review the diff with
  ''Show changes'', adjust anything you like, and save. Edits are made under your
  own account name.

=== Finding articles ===

Only a small fraction of articles have available suggestions, so the panel also lists
'''Other articles with BooksUp suggestions available''':

* '''Random''' — jump to a random article that has suggestions.
* '''My watchlist''' — list the articles on your watchlist that have suggestions.
* '''Browse worklist''' — the full list, on [https://bup.toolforge.org bup.toolforge.org].

Choosing a random or watchlist article takes you there with the panel already open.

== Notes ==

* Nothing is saved automatically — every change goes through the edit window for
  your review first, and is attributed to your account.
* The underlying list is refreshed periodically, so a suggestion may occasionally
  no longer apply, and new suggestions may become available later.

== See also ==

* [https://bup.toolforge.org The bup tool]
* [https://github.com/greencardamom/bup Source code and documentation]

[[Category:Wikipedia user scripts]]
